content:
# Exodus Chapter 30 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~51v–52r (PDF pp. 102–104); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 73–74 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_030_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_030_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_030_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_030_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE SACRED MATERIALS OF WORSHIP:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 30 across two folio pages in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter gathers five distinct prescriptions — the incense altar (vv. 1-10), the census ransom (vv. 11-16), the bronze basin (vv. 17-21), the anointing oil (vv. 22-33), and the sacred incense (vv. 34-38) — each introduced by YHWH speaking to Moses. Where chapters 25-29 built the structure and consecrated its priests, chapter 30 provides the remaining instruments and substances without which the tabernacle cannot function. The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 73-74) follows the MT sequence closely, though the LXX chapter division places some of this material differently in certain witnesses.

---

## The Altar of Incense (vv. 1–10)

**1** And you shall make [ve-asita] an altar [mizbeakh], a place of burning [miqtar] incense [qetoret]; of acacia [shittim] wood [atsei] you shall make [ta'aseh] it [oto].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ מִזְבֵּחַ מִקְטַר קְטֹרֶת עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתוֹ. **Key terms:** מִקְטַר קְטֹרֶת (miqtar qetoret) = "a place of burning incense" — miqtar is a noun of place from קטר (qatar, "to turn to smoke"); this altar's sole function is the burning of aromatic incense before YHWH.

**2** A cubit [ammah] its length [orkho] and a cubit [ve-ammah] its width [rokhbo] — square [ravua'] it shall be [yihyeh] — and two cubits [ve-ammatayim] its height [qomato]; from it [mimmenu] shall be its horns [qarnotav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אַמָּה אָרְכּוֹ וְאַמָּה רָחְבּוֹ רָבוּעַ יִהְיֶה וְאַמָּתַיִם קֹמָתוֹ מִמֶּנּוּ קַרְנֹתָיו. **Key terms:** מִמֶּנּוּ קַרְנֹתָיו (mimmenu qarnotav) = "from it, its horns" — the horns are integral to the altar, carved from the same piece of wood, not attached separately.

**3** And you shall overlay [ve-tsippita] it [oto] with pure [tahor] gold [zahav] — its top [et-gago] and its walls [ve-et-qirotav] around [saviv] and its horns [ve-et-qarnotav] — and you shall make [ve-asita] for it [lo] a molding [zer] of gold [zahav] around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתוֹ זָהָב טָהוֹר אֶת־גַּגּוֹ וְאֶת־קִירֹתָיו סָבִיב וְאֶת־קַרְנֹתָיו וְעָשִׂיתָ לּוֹ זֵר זָהָב סָבִיב. **Key terms:** זֵר (zer) = "molding, border crown" — a decorative rim of gold encircling the altar; the same term used for the ark (25:11) and the table (25:24).

**4** And two [u-shtei] rings [tabba'ot] of gold [zahav] you shall make [ta'aseh] for it [lo] beneath [mi-takhat] its molding [le-zero]; upon [al] its two [shnei] sides [tsalla'ov] you shall make [ta'aseh] them on its two [al-shnei] flanks [tsiddav]; and it shall be [ve-hayah] as holders [le-vattim] for poles [le-vaddim] to carry [laset] it [oto] with them [ba-hemah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּשְׁתֵּי טַבְּעֹת זָהָב תַּעֲשֶׂה־לּוֹ מִתַּחַת לְזֵרוֹ עַל שְׁנֵי צַלְעֹתָיו תַּעֲשֶׂה עַל־שְׁנֵי צִדָּיו וְהָיָה לְבָתִּים לְבַדִּים לָשֵׂאת אֹתוֹ בָּהֵמָּה. **Key terms:** לְבָתִּים (le-vattim) = "as holders, as housings" — from בַּיִת (bayit, "house"); the rings serve as receptacles for the carrying poles.

**5** And you shall make [ve-asita] the poles [et-ha-baddim] of acacia [shittim] wood [atsei], and you shall overlay [ve-tsippita] them [otam] with gold [zahav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֶת־הַבַּדִּים עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתָם זָהָב. **Key terms:** בַּדִּים (baddim) = "poles, staves" — the same carrying apparatus specified for the ark (25:13) and the table (25:28).

**6** And you shall put [ve-natattah] it [oto] before [lifnei] the veil [ha-parokhet] which [asher] is over [al] the ark [aron] of the testimony [ha-edut], before [lifnei] the mercy seat [ha-kapporet] which [asher] is over [al] the testimony [ha-edut], where [asher] I will meet [ivvad] with you [lekha] there [shammah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּה אֹתוֹ לִפְנֵי הַפָּרֹכֶת אֲשֶׁר עַל־אֲרֹן הָעֵדֻת לִפְנֵי הַכַּפֹּרֶת אֲשֶׁר עַל־הָעֵדֻת אֲשֶׁר אִוָּעֵד לְךָ שָׁמָּה. **Key terms:** הַכַּפֹּרֶת (ha-kapporet) = "the mercy seat, the cover of atonement" — from כפר (kaphar, "to cover, atone"); the incense altar stands just outside the veil, directly before the place of divine meeting.

**7** And Aaron [ve-hiqtir] shall burn [alav] upon it [Aharon] incense [qetoret] of spices [sammim]; in the morning [ba-boqer ba-boqer], when he tends [be-heitivo] the lamps [et-ha-nerot], he shall burn it [yaqtirenah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהִקְטִיר עָלָיו אַהֲרֹן קְטֹרֶת סַמִּים בַּבֹּקֶר בַּבֹּקֶר בְּהֵיטִיבוֹ אֶת־הַנֵּרֹת יַקְטִירֶנָּה. **Key terms:** בַּבֹּקֶר בַּבֹּקֶר (ba-boqer ba-boqer) = "in the morning, in the morning" — the Hebrew doubles the word for emphasis, meaning "every morning, morning by morning"; the incense offering is synchronized with lamp-tending.

**8** And when Aaron [u-ve-ha'alot Aharon] sets up [et-ha-nerot] the lamps [bein] between [ha-arbayim] the evenings, he shall burn it [yaqtirenah] — incense [qetoret] of continual regularity [tamid] before [lifnei] YHWH throughout your generations [le-doroteikhem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְהַעֲלֹת אַהֲרֹן אֶת־הַנֵּרֹת בֵּין הָעַרְבַּיִם יַקְטִירֶנָּה קְטֹרֶת תָּמִיד לִפְנֵי יְהוָה לְדֹרֹתֵיכֶם. **Key terms:** קְטֹרֶת תָּמִיד (qetoret tamid) = "incense of continual regularity" — tamid marks this as a perpetual ordinance, parallel to the olat tamid ("continual burnt offering") of 29:42.

**9** You shall not offer [lo-ta'alu] upon it [alav] strange [zarah] incense [qetoret], and not [ve-lo] a burnt offering [olah], and not [u-minkhah] a grain offering; and a drink offering [ve-nesekh] you shall not pour [lo] upon it [tissekhu alav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא־תַעֲלוּ עָלָיו קְטֹרֶת זָרָה וְעֹלָה וּמִנְחָה וְנֵסֶךְ לֹא תִסְּכוּ עָלָיו. **Key terms:** זָרָה (zarah) = "strange, foreign, unauthorized" — from זור (zur, "to be a stranger"); any incense other than the prescribed formula (vv. 34-38) is zarah; the prohibition is absolute and will be violated fatally by Nadab and Abihu (Lev 10:1).

**10** And Aaron [ve-khipper Aharon] shall make atonement [al] upon [qarnotav] its horns [akhat] once [ba-shanah] in the year [mi-dam]; from the blood [khattat] of the sin offering [ha-kippurim] of atonement [akhat ba-shanah] once in the year [yekhapper] he shall make atonement [alav] upon it throughout your generations [le-doroteikhem]; holy [qodesh] of holies [qodashim] it [hu] is to YHWH [la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְכִפֶּר אַהֲרֹן עַל־קַרְנֹתָיו אַחַת בַּשָּׁנָה מִדַּם חַטַּאת הַכִּפֻּרִים אַחַת בַּשָּׁנָה יְכַפֵּר עָלָיו לְדֹרֹתֵיכֶם קֹדֶשׁ־קָדָשִׁים הוּא לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** חַטַּאת הַכִּפֻּרִים (khattat ha-kippurim) = "the sin offering of atonements" — this refers to Yom Kippur (the Day of Atonement, Lev 16); once per year the incense altar's horns are purified with blood, linking this small golden altar to Israel's most solemn day.

---

## The Census Ransom — The Half-Shekel (vv. 11–16)

**11** And YHWH [va-yedabber YHWH] spoke [el-Mosheh] to Moses, saying [le'mor]:

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה לֵּאמֹר. **Key terms:** וַיְדַבֵּר (va-yedabber) = "and he spoke" — a fresh divine speech formula, marking a new section; YHWH introduces the census tax as a separate instruction.

**12** When [ki] you take up [tissa] the head [et-rosh] of the sons of Israel [benei-Yisrael] according to their counted ones [li-fequdeihem], and they shall give [ve-natenu] — each man [ish] — a ransom [kofer] of his soul [nafsho] to YHWH [la-YHWH] when counting [bi-fqod] them [otam], and there shall not be [ve-lo-yihyeh] among them [vahem] a plague [negef] when counting [bi-fqod] them [otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי תִשָּׂא אֶת־רֹאשׁ בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל לִפְקֻדֵיהֶם וְנָתְנוּ אִישׁ כֹּפֶר נַפְשׁוֹ לַיהוָה בִּפְקֹד אֹתָם וְלֹא־יִהְיֶה בָהֶם נֶגֶף בִּפְקֹד אֹתָם. **Key terms:** כֹּפֶר נַפְשׁוֹ (kofer nafsho) = "a ransom of his soul/life" — from כפר (kaphar, "to cover, ransom"); counting people exposes them to danger; the kofer is a protective payment that covers each life before YHWH.

**13** This [zeh] is what they shall give [yittenu] — everyone [kol] passing over [ha-over] among [al] the counted ones [ha-pequdim] — a half [makhatsit] of the shekel [ha-sheqel] by the shekel [be-sheqel] of the holy place [ha-qodesh]; twenty [esrim] gerahs [gerah] is the shekel [ha-sheqel]; a half [makhatsit] of the shekel [ha-sheqel] is the contribution [terumah] to YHWH [la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads זֶה יִתְּנוּ כָּל־הָעֹבֵר עַל־הַפְּקֻדִים מַחֲצִית הַשֶּׁקֶל בְּשֶׁקֶל הַקֹּדֶשׁ עֶשְׂרִים גֵּרָה הַשֶּׁקֶל מַחֲצִית הַשֶּׁקֶל תְּרוּמָה לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** תְּרוּמָה (terumah) = "contribution, heave offering" — from רום (rum, "to be high, to lift up"); the half-shekel is literally "lifted up" to YHWH as sacred donation.

**14** Everyone [kol] passing over [ha-over] among [al] the counted ones [ha-pequdim], from a son [mi-ben] of twenty [esrim] years [shanah] and upward [va-ma'lah], shall give [yitten] the contribution [terumat] of YHWH [YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כֹּל הָעֹבֵר עַל־הַפְּקֻדִים מִבֶּן עֶשְׂרִים שָׁנָה וָמָעְלָה יִתֵּן תְּרוּמַת יְהוָה. **Key terms:** מִבֶּן עֶשְׂרִים שָׁנָה (mi-ben esrim shanah) = "from a son of twenty years" — the age of military and civic responsibility; the same threshold for the census in Numbers 1:3.

**15** The rich [he-ashir] shall not give more [lo-yarbeh], and the poor [ve-ha-dal] shall not give less [lo yam'it] than the half [mi-makhatsit] of the shekel [ha-sheqel], to give [latet] the contribution [et-terumat] of YHWH [YHWH] to make atonement [le-khapper] for [al] your souls [nafshoteikhem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הֶעָשִׁיר לֹא־יַרְבֶּה וְהַדַּל לֹא יַמְעִיט מִמַּחֲצִית הַשָּׁקֶל לָתֵת אֶת־תְּרוּמַת יְהוָה לְכַפֵּר עַל־נַפְשֹׁתֵיכֶם. **Key terms:** הֶעָשִׁיר... וְהַדַּל (he-ashir... ve-ha-dal) = "the rich... and the poor" — every soul is of equal value before YHWH; the ransom is the same regardless of wealth.

**16** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] the silver [et-kesef] of the atonements [ha-kippurim] from [me-et] the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael], and you shall give [ve-natattah] it [oto] for [al] the service [avodat] of the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed]; and it shall be [ve-hayah] for the sons of Israel [li-vnei Yisrael] as a memorial [le-zikkaron] before [lifnei] YHWH, to make atonement [le-khapper] for [al] your souls [nafshoteikhem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֶת־כֶּסֶף הַכִּפֻּרִים מֵאֵת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְנָתַתָּ אֹתוֹ עַל־עֲבֹדַת אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד וְהָיָה לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לְזִכָּרוֹן לִפְנֵי יְהוָה לְכַפֵּר עַל־נַפְשֹׁתֵיכֶם. **Key terms:** לְזִכָּרוֹן (le-zikkaron) = "as a memorial" — from זכר (zakhar, "to remember"); the silver funds the tabernacle service while also serving as a remembrance before YHWH of each counted soul.

---

## The Bronze Basin for Washing (vv. 17–21)

**17** And YHWH [va-yedabber YHWH] spoke [el-Mosheh] to Moses, saying [le'mor]:

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה לֵּאמֹר. **Key terms:** A third divine speech formula in this chapter, introducing the bronze basin — another item not included in the original tabernacle instructions of chapters 25-27.

**18** And you shall make [ve-asita] a basin [kiyyor] of bronze [nekhoshet] and its stand [ve-khanno] of bronze [nekhoshet], for washing [le-rakhtsah]; and you shall put [ve-natattah] it [oto] between [bein] the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed] and between [u-vein] the altar [ha-mizbeakh], and you shall put [ve-natattah] water [mayim] there [shammah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ כִּיּוֹר נְחֹשֶׁת וְכַנּוֹ נְחֹשֶׁת לְרָחְצָה וְנָתַתָּ אֹתוֹ בֵּין־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד וּבֵין הַמִּזְבֵּחַ וְנָתַתָּ שָׁמָּה מָיִם. **Key terms:** כִּיּוֹר (kiyyor) = "basin, laver" — a large vessel for ritual washing; positioned between the tent of meeting and the altar so that priests must wash before entering either the holy place or approaching the altar.

**19** And Aaron [ve-rakhatsu] and his sons [Aharon u-vanav] shall wash [mimmenu] from it [et-yedeihem] their hands [ve-et-ragleihem] and their feet.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְרָחֲצוּ אַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו מִמֶּנּוּ אֶת־יְדֵיהֶם וְאֶת־רַגְלֵיהֶם. **Key terms:** אֶת־יְדֵיהֶם וְאֶת־רַגְלֵיהֶם (et-yedeihem ve-et-ragleihem) = "their hands and their feet" — the extremities that handle sacred objects and walk on holy ground; washing sanctifies the points of contact with the holy.

**20** When they go [be-vo'am] into [el] the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed] they shall wash [yirkhatsu] with water [mayim] and they shall not die [ve-lo yamutu]; or [o] when they draw near [be-gishtem] to [el] the altar [ha-mizbeakh] to minister [le-sharet], to burn [le-haqtir] an offering by fire [ishsheh] to YHWH [la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads בְּבֹאָם אֶל־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד יִרְחֲצוּ־מַיִם וְלֹא יָמֻתוּ אוֹ בְגִשְׁתָּם אֶל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ לְשָׁרֵת לְהַקְטִיר אִשֶּׁה לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** וְלֹא יָמֻתוּ (ve-lo yamutu) = "and they shall not die" — the death penalty for unwashed approach underscores that holiness is not merely ritual preference but a matter of life and death.

**21** And they shall wash [ve-rakhatsu] their hands [yedeihem] and their feet [ve-ragleihem], and they shall not die [ve-lo yamutu]; and it shall be [ve-hayetah] for them [lahem] a statute [khoq] of perpetuity [olam] — for him [lo] and for his seed [u-le-zar'o] throughout their generations [le-dorotam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְרָחֲצוּ יְדֵיהֶם וְרַגְלֵיהֶם וְלֹא יָמֻתוּ וְהָיְתָה לָהֶם חָק־עוֹלָם לוֹ וּלְזַרְעוֹ לְדֹרֹתָם. **Key terms:** חָק־עוֹלָם (khoq-olam) = "a statute of perpetuity" — the washing requirement is permanent, binding on all priestly generations; the bronze basin is not optional equipment.

---

## The Holy Anointing Oil (vv. 22–33)

**22** And YHWH [va-yedabber YHWH] spoke [el-Mosheh] to Moses, saying [le'mor]:

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה לֵּאמֹר. **Key terms:** A fourth divine speech formula — introducing the sacred anointing oil recipe, the most closely guarded compound in Israelite worship.

**23** And you [ve-attah] — take [qakh] for yourself [lekha] spices [besamim] of the head [rosh]: myrrh [mor] of freedom [deror] — five hundred [khamesh me'ot]; and cinnamon [ve-qinnemon] of spice [besem] — half of it [makhatsito] — fifty [khamishim] and two hundred [u-matayim]; and calamus [u-qeneh] of spice [vosem] — fifty [khamishim] and two hundred [u-matayim];

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַתָּה קַח־לְךָ בְּשָׂמִים רֹאשׁ מָר־דְּרוֹר חֲמֵשׁ מֵאוֹת וְקִנְּמָן־בֶּשֶׂם מַחֲצִיתוֹ חֲמִשִּׁים וּמָאתָיִם וּקְנֵה־בֹשֶׂם חֲמִשִּׁים וּמָאתָיִם. **Key terms:** מָר־דְּרוֹר (mor-deror) = "myrrh of freedom/flowing myrrh" — deror means "liberty, free-flowing"; this is the finest myrrh that flows freely from the tree without incision, the most precious grade.

**24** And cassia [ve-qiddah] — five hundred [khamesh me'ot] by the shekel [be-sheqel] of the holy place [ha-qodesh]; and oil [ve-shemen] of olive [zayit] — a hin [hin].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְקִדָּה חֲמֵשׁ מֵאוֹת בְּשֶׁקֶל הַקֹּדֶשׁ וְשֶׁמֶן זַיִת הִין. **Key terms:** הִין (hin) = a liquid measure of approximately 6 liters (1.5 gallons); the olive oil serves as the base into which the dry spices are compounded.

**25** And you shall make [ve-asita] it [oto] an oil [shemen] of holy [qodesh] anointing [mishkhat], a compound [roqakh] of compounding [mirqakhat], the work [ma'aseh] of a compounder [roqeakh]; an oil [shemen] of holy [qodesh] anointing [mishkhat] it shall be [yihyeh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֹתוֹ שֶׁמֶן מִשְׁחַת־קֹדֶשׁ רֹקַח מִרְקַחַת מַעֲשֵׂה רֹקֵחַ שֶׁמֶן מִשְׁחַת־קֹדֶשׁ יִהְיֶה. **Key terms:** רֹקֵחַ (roqeakh) = "compounder, perfumer" — from רקח (raqakh, "to mix spices, compound"); a skilled trade; the anointing oil requires professional preparation, not casual mixing.

**26** And you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] with it [vo] the tent [et-ohel] of meeting [mo'ed] and the ark [ve-et aron] of the testimony [ha-edut],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּמָשַׁחְתָּ בוֹ אֶת־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד וְאֵת אֲרוֹן הָעֵדֻת. **Key terms:** וּמָשַׁחְתָּ (u-mashakhta) = qal perfect of משׁח (mashakh, "to smear, anoint") — the verb from which mashiakh ("messiah, anointed one") derives; every object touched by this oil is set apart for YHWH.

**27** And the table [ve-et-ha-shulkhan] and all [ve-et-kol] its vessels [kelav], and the lampstand [ve-et-ha-menorah] and its vessels [ve-et-keleiha], and the altar [ve-et mizbeakh] of incense [ha-qetoret],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־הַשֻּׁלְחָן וְאֶת־כָּל־כֵּלָיו וְאֶת־הַמְּנֹרָה וְאֶת־כֵּלֶיהָ וְאֵת מִזְבַּח הַקְּטֹרֶת. **Key terms:** The anointing extends to every major furnishing — table, lampstand, incense altar — consecrating the entire interior space.

**28** And the altar [ve-et mizbeakh] of the burnt offering [ha-olah] and all [ve-et-kol] its vessels [kelav], and the basin [ve-et-ha-kiyyor] and its stand [ve-et-kanno].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־מִזְבַּח הָעֹלָה וְאֶת־כָּל־כֵּלָיו וְאֶת־הַכִּיֹּר וְאֶת־כַּנּוֹ. **Key terms:** The anointing continues outward to the courtyard furnishings — the bronze altar and the bronze basin — encompassing the entire sacred precinct.

**29** And you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] them [otam], and they shall be [ve-hayu] holy [qodesh] of holies [qodashim]; everything [kol] touching [ha-nogea'] them [bahem] shall be holy [yiqdash].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ אֹתָם וְהָיוּ קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים כָּל־הַנֹּגֵעַ בָּהֶם יִקְדָּשׁ. **Key terms:** כָּל־הַנֹּגֵעַ בָּהֶם יִקְדָּשׁ (kol-ha-nogea' bahem yiqdash) = "everything touching them shall be holy" — holiness is contagious through contact; the anointed objects transmit their consecrated status.

**30** And Aaron [ve-et-Aharon] and his sons [ve-et-banav] you shall anoint [timshakh], and you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] them [otam] to be priests [le-khahen] to me [li].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־אַהֲרֹן וְאֶת־בָּנָיו תִּמְשָׁח וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ אֹתָם לְכַהֵן לִי. **Key terms:** תִּמְשָׁח (timshakh) = qal imperfect of משׁח — "you shall anoint"; the same oil consecrates both objects and persons; priests and furniture alike are set apart by the same substance.

**31** And to [ve-el] the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael] you shall speak [tedabber], saying [le'mor]: An oil [shemen] of holy [qodesh] anointing [mishkhat] this [zeh] shall be [yihyeh] to me [li] throughout your generations [le-doroteikhem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל תְּדַבֵּר לֵאמֹר שֶׁמֶן מִשְׁחַת־קֹדֶשׁ יִהְיֶה זֶה לִי לְדֹרֹתֵיכֶם. **Key terms:** לִי (li) = "to me, for me" — the oil belongs to YHWH; it is not communal property but a divine possession entrusted for specific use.

**32** Upon [al] the flesh [besar] of a man [adam] it shall not be poured [lo yissakh], and in its proportion [u-ve-matkunato] you shall not make [lo ta'asu] like it [kamohu]; holy [qodesh] it [hu] is — holy [qodesh] it shall be [yihyeh] to you [lakhem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads עַל־בְּשַׂר אָדָם לֹא יִיסָּךְ וּבְמַתְכֻּנְתּוֹ לֹא תַעֲשׂוּ כָּמֹהוּ קֹדֶשׁ הוּא קֹדֶשׁ יִהְיֶה לָכֶם. **Key terms:** מַתְכֻּנְתּוֹ (matkunato) = "its proportion, its formula" — from תכן (takhan, "to measure, regulate"); the exact recipe is restricted; no one may duplicate the proportions for personal use.

**33** A man [ish] who [asher] compounds [yirqakh] like it [kamohu] and who [va-asher] puts [yitten] from it [mimmenu] upon [al] a stranger [zar] shall be cut off [ve-nikhrat] from his people [me-ammav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אִישׁ אֲשֶׁר יִרְקַח כָּמֹהוּ וַאֲשֶׁר יִתֵּן מִמֶּנּוּ עַל־זָר וְנִכְרַת מֵעַמָּיו. **Key terms:** וְנִכְרַת (ve-nikhrat) = niphal perfect of כרת (karat, "to cut off") — the penalty of karet, excision from the community; whether this means death, exile, or divine punishment is debated, but it is the most severe penalty for ritual violation.

---

## The Holy Incense (vv. 34–38)

**34** And YHWH [va-yomer YHWH] said [el-Mosheh] to Moses: Take [qakh] for yourself [lekha] spices [sammim] — stacte [nataf] and onycha [u-shkhelet] and galbanum [ve-khelbenah] — spices [sammim] and pure [zakh] frankincense [u-levonah]; part [bad] for part [be-vad] they shall be [yihyeh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה קַח־לְךָ סַמִּים נָטָף וּשְׁחֵלֶת וְחֶלְבְּנָה סַמִּים וּלְבֹנָה זַכָּה בַּד בְּבַד יִהְיֶה. **Key terms:** בַּד בְּבַד (bad be-vad) = "part for part, equal portions" — each ingredient in equal measure; the incense formula requires precise balance among its four components.

**35** And you shall make [ve-asita] it [otah] an incense [qetoret], a compound [roqakh], the work [ma'aseh] of a compounder [roqeakh], salted [memullakh], pure [tahor], holy [qodesh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֹתָהּ קְטֹרֶת רֹקַח מַעֲשֵׂה רוֹקֵחַ מְמֻלָּח טָהוֹר קֹדֶשׁ. **Key terms:** מְמֻלָּח (memullakh) = "salted, well-mixed" — from מלח (malakh, "to salt"); salt was a preservative and a sign of covenant (Lev 2:13); its inclusion marks the incense as a covenant substance.

**36** And you shall beat [ve-shakhaqta] some of it [mimmennah] fine [hadeq], and you shall put [ve-natattah] some of it [mimmennah] before [lifnei] the testimony [ha-edut] in the tent [be-ohel] of meeting [mo'ed], where [asher] I will meet [ivvad] with you [lekha] there [shammah]; holy [qodesh] of holies [qodashim] it shall be [tihyeh] to you [lakhem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁחַקְתָּ מִמֶּנָּה הָדֵק וְנָתַתָּה מִמֶּנָּה לִפְנֵי הָעֵדֻת בְּאֹהֶל מוֹעֵד אֲשֶׁר אִוָּעֵד לְךָ שָׁמָּה קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים תִּהְיֶה לָכֶם. **Key terms:** וְשָׁחַקְתָּ... הָדֵק (ve-shakhaqta... hadeq) = "and you shall beat... fine" — the incense is ground to a fine powder before use; hadeq is an adjective meaning "thin, fine," emphasizing the meticulous preparation required.

**37** And the incense [ve-ha-qetoret] which [asher] you shall make [ta'aseh] — in its proportion [be-matkunatah] you shall not make [lo ta'asu] it for yourselves [lakhem]; holy [qodesh] it shall be [tihyeh] to you [lekha] to YHWH [la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַקְּטֹרֶת אֲשֶׁר תַּעֲשֶׂה בְּמַתְכֻּנְתָּהּ לֹא תַעֲשׂוּ לָכֶם קֹדֶשׁ תִּהְיֶה לְךָ לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** בְּמַתְכֻּנְתָּהּ (be-matkunatah) = "in its proportion" — the same term as v. 32 for the oil; the incense formula, like the oil formula, is exclusively sacred and may not be replicated for personal enjoyment.

**38** A man [ish] who [asher] makes [ya'aseh] like it [kamohah] to smell [le-hariakh] it [bah] shall be cut off [ve-nikhrat] from his people [me-ammav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אִישׁ אֲשֶׁר־יַעֲשֶׂה כָמוֹהָ לְהָרִיחַ בָּהּ וְנִכְרַת מֵעַמָּיו. **Key terms:** לְהָרִיחַ (le-hariakh) = "to smell, to enjoy the scent" — hiphil infinitive construct of ריח (riakh, "to smell"); the offense is specifically making the incense for personal sensory pleasure; what belongs to YHWH's nostrils is forbidden to human enjoyment.

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 30 is a chapter of sacred recipes and protective boundaries. Its five sections — incense altar, census ransom, bronze basin, anointing oil, and sacred incense — supply the remaining elements without which the tabernacle system cannot operate. Where chapters 25-29 provided the structure, furniture, garments, and priestly consecration, chapter 30 fills in what was deferred: the substances and instruments of ongoing worship.

The incense altar (vv. 1-10) is notably separated from the other furniture instructions of chapters 25-27. Its placement here, after the priestly consecration of chapter 29, has generated much discussion. The altar stands before the veil — not inside the Holy of Holies, yet oriented toward the kapporet — occupying a liminal position between the accessible and the unapproachable. Its annual blood-purification on Yom Kippur (v. 10) links this small golden altar to Israel's most solemn day, when the boundary between outer and inner sanctuary is temporarily crossed.

The census ransom (vv. 11-16) introduces the theological danger of counting. The kofer nafsho ("ransom of his soul") reveals that numbering people is not a neutral administrative act but an exposure of each life to divine scrutiny. The half-shekel — identical for rich and poor — declares the equal value of every soul before YHWH. The silver funds the tabernacle service while simultaneously serving as zikkaron ("memorial"), a perpetual reminder of lives ransomed.

The bronze basin (vv. 17-21) is sparse in description but absolute in consequence: ve-lo yamutu ("and they shall not die"). Unwashed hands and feet approaching the holy place or the altar mean death. The basin stands between tent and altar as an unavoidable threshold — the priests cannot reach either sacred site without passing through water.

The anointing oil (vv. 22-33) and the sacred incense (vv. 34-38) form a paired conclusion, each with its precise recipe, its exclusive sacred purpose, and its karet penalty for unauthorized reproduction. The oil recipe — flowing myrrh, cinnamon, calamus, cassia in olive oil — and the incense formula — stacte, onycha, galbanum, and frankincense in equal parts — are the only detailed compound recipes in the Torah. Both are protected by the strongest penalty short of execution: ve-nikhrat me-ammav ("and he shall be cut off from his people"). These formulas belong to YHWH alone; to duplicate them for human pleasure is to collapse the boundary between sacred and common.

Key MT-LXX divergences: (1) The Vaticanus text follows the MT sequence closely throughout this chapter. (2) The LXX renders kofer nafsho (v. 12) as λύτρα τῆς ψυχῆς ("ransom of the soul"), preserving the Hebrew concept. (3) The spice terms in vv. 23-24 and 34 show varied Greek renderings: mor-deror becomes σμύρνα ἐκλεκτή ("choice myrrh") in the LXX, and khelbenah becomes χαλβάνη. (4) The LXX renders bad be-vad (v. 34) as ἴσον ἴσῳ ("equal to equal"), accurately conveying the equal-parts requirement. (5) The karet formula ve-nikhrat me-ammav is rendered ἐξολεθρευθήσεται ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτοῦ ("shall be utterly destroyed from his people") in both v. 33 and v. 38, using a stronger Greek verb than simple "cutting off." (6) The LXX consistently renders YHWH as κύριος throughout.
